data.narrative:
X-Bow Systems, a Series B defense and security company founded by Jason Hundley, Mark Kaufman, and Maureen Gannon, has raised $157M to date. The valuation analysis references a comparable universe of 10 companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms at the same stage and sector, including Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Code Metal, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $157M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $298M. Peer-median analysis examines seven same-sector, same-stage comparables with valuations ranging from $300M to $3.0B; the median valuation across this set is $1.0B. The peer distribution reflects meaningful dispersion, reflecting differences in technology maturity, customer traction, or funding efficiency within the defense and security segment.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $254M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median implied value of $649M. The $395M spread between low and high scenarios reflects the tension between capital-efficiency benchmarking (stage-progression) and peer comparables, which skew higher. Key uncertainties include X-Bow's relative position within the peer set on revenue, customer concentration, and technology differentiation—factors not available in the current dataset.
